items.title: Pauline Hanson's criticism of Guardian reporter labelled an 'assault on free press'

children.content: Pauline Hanson's public excoriation of a Guardian journalist questioning her at the National Press Club yesterday is an "assault on the freedom of the press", the media union says, as Labor and Liberal MPs criticise the One Nation leader for getting personal with journalists.

articles.title: One Nation prepared to cut worker entitlements to shore up struggling small businesses

children.content: One Nation's newly named "treasury spokesman" Barnaby Joyce says the party is prepared to cut worker entitlements to safeguard small businesses struggling to deal with inflation and high power prices.

image.altText: A man and a woman stand next to each other.

articles.title: Hanson wasn't there for Press Club. She was there for Fight Club

children.content: The spectacle of the One Nation leader having a blue with journalists — or indeed anyone even vaguely associated with Canberra incumbency — is catnip for the Hanson algorithm. And the lady is not stupid.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson stands at the lectern at the National Press Club, a serious expression on her face

avatar.alt: A close up photo of Annabel Crabb, who has long brown hair and is wearing red spectacles, red lipstick and a blue top

items.title: Hanson's fiery address creates risk for One Nation

children.content: In one press club appearance, we've learned more about where Hanson stands. And that creates risk for One Nation.

image.altText: A woman with red hair speaks at a lectern.

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Couple left with nothing after investing $1m in 7-Eleven franchise

children.content: Jotika and Sunny Sharma took out a loan to purchase their Sydney franchise in 2015. When the lease expired this year, their relationship with the convenience store giant turned ugly.

image.altText: Couple look at the camera, both with feelings of distress on their faces.

items.title: US releases text of ceasefire deal struck with Iran

children.content: The official text of the memorandum of understanding includes American commitments to remove sanctions imposed on Tehran while Iran will agree to dilute its enriched uranium stockpile.

gallery.altText: A close up of Donald Trump's face in front of an American flag.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of Donald Trump's face in front of an American flag.

items.title: Electricity like milk? The plan to simplify complex power bills

children.content: Millions of Australians have been often unwittingly moved to complex and variable power tariffs. An energy watchdog says bills must be simplified.

image.altText: Shot of a smart meter in its box on the side of a house

items.title: Wife and carer 'dumbfounded' by husband's aged care assessment

children.content: Melbourne man Graham Crossan relies on his wife Gaynor to help him eat, move and even breathe. But a new assessment tool means he's been refused funding for more support.

image.altText: A woman with light blonde hair in a pink shirt rests her hand on the head of a bald man in a chair with an oxygen mask on.

articles.title: What to know about the Socceroos's next World Cup match

children.content: The Socceroos will face the USA in their second Group D match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Here's what time you'll need to set your alarm.

image.altText: Connor Metcalfe and Nishan Velupillay hug on the pitch.

articles.title: Quick hits: Mbappé's stunning goal and why this coach refused to pose

children.content: Kylian Mbappé's magical strike thrust him back into the World Cup spotlight, while master coach Marcelo Bielsa did everything he could to avoid it. French journalists leave an empty space and Jesus gets a Mexican kit makeover. Here's the five quick hits from the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Kylian Mbappé, Roberto Lopes and Marcelo Bielsa composite.

children.content: England kicks off its campaign against Croatia, the team that beat the Three Lions at the semifinal stage in 2018, after Ronaldo stumbles as Portugal drew with DR Congo. Follow live.

items.title: England scores retaken penalty to lead Croatia, DR Congo holds Portugal
KPMG the latest example of accountants being unaccountable

children.content: Bean counters have historically been viewed as dull, dull, dull. But since the turn of the century, accounting scandals have come thick and fast in Australia.

image.altText: People silhouetted against a white and blue KPMG logo.

items.title: HSBC fought customers 'tooth and nail' over scam but now it's fronting court

children.content: HSBC customers who became victims of a long-running "spoofing" scam hope the bank will take full responsibility for its "system failures" in Federal Court this week.

image.altText: A woman with long black hair and a white shirt looks at camera.

items.title: There's blood in Blue water after NSW collapse in Origin II

children.content: New South Wales travelled to the MCG with the Origin series within their grasp but after the disaster that ensued success has never felt further away.

articles.title: Quick hits: Munster's HIA miracle and Klein listens to his bunker

children.content: Billy Slater resists the urge to play his trump card as the Maroons recover from an inexcusable early blunder. Here are five quick hits from State of Origin II.

image.altText: SOOII

articles.title: Maroons maul Blues in Melbourne to send Origin series to a decider

children.content: Queensland ignites with 36 points in the second half of State of Origin II to beat the Blues 44-24 and send the series to a decider.

items.title: Homeless woman feels safer living in vehicle than shared housing

children.content: Housing advocates say an increasing number of single, older women are choosing to live in vehicles while they wait for social housing.

items.title: Don't expect a post-budget $1,250 boost this tax time

children.content: There was a lot of talk about the $1,000 instant tax reduction and the $250 Working Australians Tax Offset in the federal budget. But they won't be in play at tax time this year.

image.altText: Woman with head in hands with tax return on screen in the background.

items.title: Uni students tackle 'character-building' remote living through placements

children.content: University student Tarhnee says her survival kit for a nursing prac placement was food, a swag, a chair and a little table for her laptop.

image.altText: A small tent and camping chair.

items.title: Sydney-to-London unveiled as first route for Qantas's Project Sunrise

children.content: The flights, set to begin take off from next year, will mark the first time flights from the east coast will complete the Kangaroo Route without a stop over.

items.title: Australian horror film Leviticus weaponises homophobia to terrifying effect

children.content: Writer and director Adrian Chiarella's debut supernatural horror feature, Leviticus, is as romantic as it is scary.

children.content: In Peru, I meet a man risking his life to end illegal gold mining in the Amazon, in the hope of saving his beloved town before it's too late.

items.title: Injured toddler's ordeal highlights risks of weather radar blackspot

children.content: When two-year-old Teddy suffered a suspected broken leg on his family's outback property, the journey to hospital was complicated by a lack of reliable weather forecasting.

items.title: Mangione to argue he suffered 'extreme emotional disturbance' in murder trial

children.content: The 28-year-old is accused of killing an American health insurance executive. If successful, his defence could see him convicted of manslaughter rather than murder.

image.altText: Luigi Mangione in a suit and shirt sitting at the defence table holding an envelope.

items.title: Underground discovery could help save one of the world's rarest mammals

children.content: One of the world's most critically endangered animals may be able to live in more areas than conservationists realised.

children.content: Ellyse Perry dominates with the ball as Australia waltzes home to victory over Bangladesh in the women's T20 World Cup.

items.title: AI factory 'brings into doubt' future of Tasmania's Marinus Link

children.content: Tasmania's cool climate and access to renewable energy has made it an attractive place for AI data centre investment, but energy industry voices are warning that the business case for the Marinus Link project appears increasingly unviable if power-hungry players such as AI data centres continue to expand across the state.

image.altText: A large data centre sits prominantly in a forested landscape at Bell Bay.

items.title: RBA warns financial industry to prepare for 'more shock-prone future'

children.content: A new era of strategic uncertainty has serious implications for Australia's financial industry, the Reserve Bank warns.

image.altText: A cyber-crime graphic.

items.title: 'Whole bloodlines' have been lost in Sudan but the world isn't watching

children.content: Sudanese Australians are kept up at night thinking of family and friends trapped as a result of one of the world's worst humanitarian crises.

image.altText: A woman dressed in white wearing a hijab scrolling through a phone.

items.title: Father of 17yo killed in e-bike crash speaks after teen driver sentenced

children.content: A Gold Coast father whose son died in an electric bike crash has spoken out after the driver was sentenced, saying the devices need better regulation.

items.title: Bill restricting late-term abortions fails to pass SA lower house

children.content: South Australia's lower house has voted against a bill to restrict access to abortions past 25 weeks. The legislation was defeated 36 votes to 9.

image.altText: A woman in a black west with a green and blue ribbon pinned to her chest walks up stone steps.

items.title: El Niño has arrived and it could be the most volatile yet

children.content: El Niño is declared and scientists say the climate change is making its impacts harder to predict.

image.altText: A man in boots standing on dusty, dry land.

items.title: Cambodia says 310,000 suspected scammers left country amid crackdown

children.content: A report by Amnesty International casts doubt on the impact of Cambodia's high-profile scam industry crackdown, finding authorities failed to dismantle the "vast majority" of operations.

items.title: 'Moment of grief': Pakistani police apologise for 9yo Australian girl's death

children.content: Pakistani officials apologise for the fatal shooting of a nine-year-old Australian girl, and outline the violation of protocols that led to her death as they pursue murder charges against the officer involved.

image.altText: Selfie of a man and his daughter on a plane.

items.title: Jeremy Clarkson reveals 'aggressive' cancer diagnosis

children.content: The former Top Gear host shared the news on an episode of his Clarkson's Farm reality series, shortly before undergoing surgery that removed "10 per cent" of his prostate.

image.altText: An older man in a chequered shirt leans back in his chair with a grim expression on his face.

items.title: Legal aid worker caught in bed with fugitive teen killer pleads guilty

children.content: A 37-year-old legal aid worker who was arrested while sharing a hotel bed with a teenage killer she helped evade authorities after he breached his parole has pleaded guilty to attempting to pervert the course of justice.

items.title: Kyle Sandilands responds to his $12m settlement with ARN Media

children.content: In an ASX announcement on Wednesday, ARN Media agreed to pay the shock jock $12.09 million, with $3 million payable as soon as next month.

items.title: Zoo raises chimp on human colostrum after mum accidentally drops newborn

children.content: Rockhampton Zoo is hand-rearing a chimp whose mother dropped it from a height by accident hours after giving birth.
